q.en: Where was Irma's sandwich shop located?
q.zh: Irma 的三明治店开在哪里？

options.en: On the corner of Pumpernickel and Rye, the busiest corner in town
options.zh: 在 Pumpernickel 街和 Rye 街的拐角，镇上最繁忙的角落

options.en: Next to the school
options.zh: 在学校旁边

options.en: In a nearby swamp
options.zh: 在附近的沼泽边

options.en: On the highway
options.zh: 在公路上

explain.en: Irma's shop was on the corner of Pumpernickel and Rye, which is described as the busiest corner in town.
explain.zh: Irma 的店开在 Pumpernickel 街和 Rye 街的拐角，书里说那是镇上最繁忙的角落。

q.en: What did the sign on Irma's shop say?
q.zh: Irma 店门口的招牌上写的是什么？

options.en: Irma's Tasty Sandwich Shop - You name it, and I make it
options.zh: Irma 的美味三明治店——你说得出，我就做得到

options.en: Open 24 hours
options.zh: 24 小时营业

options.en: Sandwiches half price
options.zh: 三明治半价

options.en: No animals allowed
options.zh: 禁止动物入内

explain.en: Irma nailed a sign to the front of her shop reading 'Irma's Tasty Sandwich Shop - You name it, and I make it.'
explain.zh: Irma 在店门口钉了一块招牌，上面写着'Irma 的美味三明治店——你说得出，我就做得到'。

q.en: Who was Irma's first customer, and what sandwich did he order?
q.zh: Irma 的第一位顾客是谁？他点了什么三明治？

options.en: Reuben Cornwall, who ordered corned beef, Swiss cheese, and sauerkraut on rye
options.zh: Reuben Cornwall，点了一份黑麦面包夹咸牛肉、瑞士奶酪和酸菜的三明治

options.en: Joey Talltree, who ordered a sausage sandwich
options.zh: Joey Talltree，点了香肠三明治

options.en: Sammy Snake, who ordered a rat sandwich
options.zh: Sammy 蛇，点了老鼠三明治

options.en: Billy the Goat, who ordered tin cans
options.zh: Billy 山羊，点了罐头

explain.en: Irma's first customer was Reuben Cornwall, who wanted corned beef with Swiss cheese and sauerkraut on rye; Irma named it a Reuben and sold more Reubens than any other sandwich.
explain.zh: Irma 的第一位顾客是 Reuben Cornwall，他想要黑麦面包夹咸牛肉、瑞士奶酪和酸菜；Irma 把它命名为 Reuben，而且 Reuben 卖得比其他任何三明治都多。

q.en: How is the book organized to help readers follow Irma's story?
q.zh: 这本书是如何组织编排，以帮助读者了解 Irma 的故事的？

options.en: It uses a Table of Contents with four sections, ending with 'Irma's Biggest Challenge'
options.zh: 它用目录分成四个部分，最后一部分是'Irma 最大的挑战'

options.en: It is written as a single long poem
options.zh: 它是一首长诗

options.en: It jumps randomly between different shops
options.zh: 它在不同的店铺之间随意跳跃

options.en: It has no chapters, just one list of ingredients
options.zh: 它没有章节，只有一串食材清单

explain.en: The book opens with a Table of Contents listing four parts: Irma Opens a Shop, Irma's Famous Sandwiches, All the Animals Want Sandwiches, and Irma's Biggest Challenge.
explain.zh: 这本书以目录开头，列出四个部分：Irma 开店、Irma 的招牌三明治、所有动物都来点三明治、Irma 最大的挑战。

q.en: How does the author create humor in the animal customers' orders?
q.zh: 作者是如何通过动物顾客的点单来制造幽默的？

options.en: By having each animal order foods that match its real diet in a silly, exaggerated way
options.zh: 让每种动物都点符合它真实食性、却又滑稽夸张的食物

options.en: By making the animals order only normal human sandwiches
options.zh: 让动物们只点普通的人类三明治

options.en: By having the animals refuse to eat anything
options.zh: 让动物们什么都不吃

options.en: By describing the animals as very quiet and polite
options.zh: 把动物描写得非常安静有礼貌

explain.en: The snake orders rats, lizards, and bugs; the vulture orders dead opossum, crow, and mouse - matching their real diets but played for laughs.
explain.zh: 蛇点了老鼠、蜥蜴和虫子；秃鹰点了死负鼠、乌鸦和老鼠——既符合它们真实的食性，又被写得滑稽好笑。

q.en: Sammy Snake's order (rat and lizard sandwich with bug relish) shows a connection to what real fact?
q.zh: Sammy 蛇点的（老鼠和蜥蜴三明治配虫子酱）与哪条真实常识相符？

options.en: Snakes in real life eat small animals like rats, lizards, and insects
options.zh: 现实中的蛇会吃老鼠、蜥蜴和昆虫等小动物

options.en: Snakes are vegetarians
options.zh: 蛇是素食动物

options.en: Snakes only eat bread
options.zh: 蛇只吃面包

options.en: Snakes drink dishwater
options.zh: 蛇喝洗碗水

explain.en: Sammy Snake orders prey animals (rats, lizards, bugs) that match what snakes really eat, linking the story to real animal behavior.
explain.zh: Sammy 蛇点的猎物（老鼠、蜥蜴、虫子）正是蛇真实会吃的东西，把故事和真实的动物习性联系了起来。

q.en: Val the Vulture orders 'fresh dead opossum, squashed crow, and rancid mouse.' This reflects vultures' real role as what kind of eater?
q.zh: Val 秃鹰点了'新鲜死负鼠、压扁的乌鸦和腐臭老鼠'。这反映了秃鹰在现实中是什么类型的食者？

options.en: Scavengers that eat dead animals (carrion)
options.zh: 吃死尸（腐肉）的清道夫

options.en: Plant eaters that live on fruit
options.zh: 以水果为生的植食动物

options.en: Hunters that catch live fish
options.zh: 捕捉活鱼的猎手

options.en: Insects that sip nectar
options.zh: 吸食花蜜的昆虫

explain.en: Vultures are scavengers that feed on dead animals, which is why Val orders dead opossum, crow, and mouse.
explain.zh: 秃鹰是吃死尸的清道夫，所以 Val 会点死负鼠、乌鸦和老鼠。

q.en: Why does the author make Irma a character who does NOT like sandwiches but LOVES making them?
q.zh: 作者为什么把 Irma 写成一个'不爱吃三明治却爱做三明治'的角色？

options.en: To create a surprising, funny contrast that makes her character interesting
options.zh: 为了制造一个出人意料、好笑的反差，让角色更有趣

options.en: To show that Irma is a bad cook
options.zh: 为了说明 Irma 是个糟糕的厨师

options.en: To prove sandwiches are unhealthy
options.zh: 为了证明三明治不健康

options.en: To explain why the shop closed
options.zh: 为了解释店铺为何关门

explain.en: The contrast - Irma dislikes eating sandwiches yet loves making them - adds humor and makes her an unusual, memorable character.
explain.zh: 这种反差——Irma 不爱吃三明治却爱做三明治——增添了幽默感，让她成为一个与众不同、令人难忘的角色。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose for writing Irma's Sandwich Shop?
q.zh: 作者写《Irma 的三明治店》的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To entertain readers with a funny, imaginative story
options.zh: 用一个滑稽、充满想象力的故事来娱乐读者

options.en: To teach a recipe for a Reuben sandwich
options.zh: 为了教做 Reuben 三明治的食谱

options.en: To explain how to run a real business
options.zh: 为了讲解如何经营真实生意

options.en: To give facts about animal habitats
options.zh: 为了提供动物栖息地的知识

explain.en: The talking animals, silly ingredients, and playful tone show the story is meant to entertain, not to teach real facts.
explain.zh: 会说话的动物、滑稽的食材和俏皮的语气都表明，这个故事是为了娱乐，而不是传授真实知识。

q.en: What makes Billy the Goat's order Irma's 'biggest challenge,' and what does Irma learn from it?
q.zh: 为什么 Billy 山羊点的单是 Irma'最大的挑战'？她从中明白了什么？

options.en: He orders strange non-food items like tin cans and old socks (plus a mug of dishwater), and after making it Irma knows there is no sandwich she can't make
options.zh: 他点了罐头、旧袜子等奇怪的非食物（外加一杯洗碗水），做完后 Irma 明白没有她做不出的三明治

options.en: He orders the most Reubens ever
options.zh: 他点了史上最多的 Reuben

options.en: He asks for a plain cheese sandwich
options.zh: 他只要一个普通奶酪三明治

options.en: He wants the sandwich delivered to the swamp
options.zh: 他想把三明治送到沼泽

explain.en: Billy orders crushed tin cans, old socks, thistles, a bird nest, an old book, cabbage, and a mug of dishwater; after making it, Irma truly knows there is no sandwich she can't make.
explain.zh: Billy 点了压碎的罐头、旧袜子、蓟草、鸟巢、旧书、卷心菜和一杯洗碗水；做完之后，Irma 真正相信没有她做不出的三明治。

prompt.en: Irma says 'You name it, and I make it.' Imagine your own unusual sandwich shop. What would your sign say, and what is the strangest sandwich you could proudly make?
prompt.zh: Irma 说'你说得出，我就做得到'。想象你自己的奇特三明治店。你的招牌会写什么？你能自豪地做出的最奇怪的三明治是什么？

guidance.en:
• Think of a silly or surprising ingredient combination that still follows a theme (like the animals' real diets).
• Give your sandwich a fun name, just as Irma named the Reuben.
• Make your shop sign short and catchy.
• Since the story is fiction, creativity matters more than a real recipe.
guidance.zh:
• 想一组滑稽或出人意料的食材搭配，最好能呼应某个主题（就像动物们真实的食性）。
• 给你的三明治起个有趣的名字，就像 Irma 给 Reuben 命名那样。
• 让你的招牌简短、好记。
• 这是虚构故事，创意比真实食谱更重要。
